contents.content: Welcome to Rico
Thank you for choosing Rico. This documentation will help you set up, customize, and manage your store using the theme.

Rico is built on Shopify Online Store 2.0 and is designed to provide a flexible and customizable foundation for a wide range of businesses. Whether you are launching a new store or updating an existing one, Rico provides the tools needed to create a professional shopping experience.
Getting Started
Before customizing your storefront, we recommend completing the basic store setup within Shopify.

items: Add products and collections.
Upload your logo and brand assets.
Configure store navigation and menus.
Review theme settings and customize sections.
Preview your store on desktop and mobile devices.
Test the customer journey before publishing.

contents.content: Customization
Rico supports Shopify's Theme Editor, allowing you to customize layouts, content, colors, typography, and sections without editing code.
Theme Compatibility
Rico is built for Shopify Online Store 2.0 and supports Shopify's modern theme architecture, including sections, app blocks, and dynamic content.
Use the navigation menu to explore additional guides and resources available throughout the documentation.
Install Theme
Add Rico to your store and preview before publishing.
You can run multiple themes in your library but only one is live at a time. Duplicating or previewing lets you test without touching the published storefront.
Try Rico free

items: In Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Add theme → Visit Theme Store.
Find Rico and click Try theme.

contents.content: Trial themes show a Theme trial label. Customizations you make during the trial carry over if you purchase.
Buy and publish

items: In Theme library, open your Rico trial.
Click Buy and complete checkout.
When ready, click Publish to make it live.

contents.content: Paid themes are licensed to the store where they were purchased. You need Themes and billing permissions to buy.
Work safely with copies

items: Duplicate your live theme before major edits.
Use preview share links to gather feedback before publishing.
Most stores can hold up to 20 themes — remove old copies if you hit the limit.

contents.content: After install, start with Editing your theme. Help and docs links are under Theme actions in the editor — see Theme support.
Editing Your Theme
Customize layout, content, and style in the theme editor — no code required.
The theme editor is a visual workspace for your templates. Changes apply to the theme you're customizing; publish when you're ready for customers to see them.
Open the editor

items: Go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the theme you want to edit.

contents.content: The left sidebar lists sections and blocks for the current template. Theme settings (gear icon) holds storewide defaults — colors, fonts, cards, and cart behavior.
Navigate templates and pages
Use the top template menu to switch pages — home, product, collection, cart, blog, and others. Click Change in the preview bar to preview with a specific product, collection, or blog post loaded.
The Header and Footer groups are global: edits there appear on every page.
Add, move, and hide content

items: Add section — use + above or below a section, or the Add section button in the sidebar.
Add block — open a section and click Add block.
Reorder — drag the handle on a section or block.
Hide — click the eye icon to hide without deleting settings.
Remove — select the item and click Remove section or Remove block.

contents.content: Click Save to apply. Undo/redo works until you save.
Use the preview inspector

Toggle the preview inspector in the top bar, then click any outlined area on the live preview to jump straight to that section or block's settings. Switch desktop, mobile, and full-screen preview icons to check responsive layouts.
Duplicate before big changes
In Online Store → Themes, click … → Duplicate on your live theme. Customize the copy, then publish when satisfied.
Storewide options live under Theme settings — start with Colors, Fonts, and Layout. For help, see Theme support.
Theme Support
Documentation, contact options, and what support covers.
Rico is a paid theme — support comes from the theme developer, not Shopify's free-theme design team.
Open docs or contact support

items: Go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on Rico.
Open Theme actions (top bar).
Choose View documentation or Get support.

contents.content: Documentation covers theme settings, sections, and common tasks. Support handles bugs, setup questions, and guidance on built-in settings.
What support covers

items: Questions about Rico settings and sections.
Troubleshooting theme behavior.
Guidance on using the theme editor.

contents.content: Support does not include custom code, third-party app integration, checkout edits, image retouching, or changes to themes with heavy code modifications.
Supported browsers

Rico targets current versions of Safari, Chrome, Firefox, and Edge on desktop, and Mobile Safari, Chrome Mobile, and Samsung Internet on mobile. Test major changes in your target browsers before publishing.
Shopify's general theme guides are available at help.shopify.com/manual/online-store/themes. For app-related issues, contact the app developer via Settings → Apps and sales channels → Get support.
Logo and Favicon
Configure your store's logo and favicon displayed across the storefront.
Logo

items: Default logo: Select the image used as your store logo in the header and other logo locations throughout the theme.
Max Width: Controls the maximum width of the logo in pixels. Increasing the value makes the logo appear larger; decreasing it makes the logo appear smaller. (Default: 72px)

contents.content: Favicon
Select the favicon displayed in browser tabs and bookmarks. For best results, use a square image sized 32 × 32 pixels.
Colors
Customize the colors used throughout your store to match your brand identity.
Color Schemes

Color schemes control the colors used throughout your store. By creating multiple color schemes, you can assign different color combinations to sections and create visual contrast while maintaining a consistent brand identity.

contents.label: How to manage color schemes
items: View or edit: Go to Theme Settings → Colors in the customizer to view, edit, or create color schemes.
Add a new scheme: Click Add Scheme to create a new color scheme. Once created, you can customize the colors and assign the scheme to supported sections.
Edit a scheme: Select an existing color scheme to modify its colors. Changes are automatically reflected in any sections using that scheme.
Apply a scheme: Color schemes can be applied to supported sections across your store — for example, a light scheme for content sections and a dark scheme for promotional banners.

contents.content: Editing a Color Scheme
To edit a color scheme, go to Theme Settings → Colors and click on the scheme you want to modify. Any changes you make are automatically applied to all sections that use that color scheme.

contents.label: Background
items: Background: The primary background color for sections using this scheme. (Default: #FFFFFF)
Foreground: The foreground surface color used within the scheme. (Default: #F2F2F2)
Background Gradient: Optional gradient that replaces the solid background color when configured.

contents.label: Text
items: Text: The primary text color. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Secondary Text: Used for supporting or less prominent text. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Link: Color used for text links. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Hover: Color used for interactive elements on hover. (Default: #1C1C1C)

contents.label: Primary Button
items: Background: Button background color. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Text: Button text color. (Default: #FFFFFF)
Border: Button border color. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Hover Background: Button background color when hovered. (Default: Transparent)
Hover Text: Button text color when hovered. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Hover Border: Button border color when hovered. (Default: #1C1C1C)

contents.label: Secondary Button
items: Background: Button background color. (Default: Transparent)
Text: Button text color. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Border: Button border color. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Hover Background: Button background color when hovered. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Hover Text: Button text color when hovered. (Default: #FFFFFF)
Hover Border: Button border color when hovered. (Default: #1C1C1C)

contents.label: Section Heading
items: Text: Section heading text color. (Default: #FFFFFF)
Background: Section heading background color. (Default: #000000)

contents.label: Tips for using color schemes
items: Create multiple schemes to give different sections their own visual style while maintaining brand consistency.
Use high contrast between text and background colors to improve readability.
Preview changes across different sections to ensure colors remain accessible and visually balanced.

contents.content: Removing a Color Scheme

To delete a color scheme, open the scheme and click Remove color scheme at the bottom of the editor. Any sections currently using that scheme should be reassigned to another color scheme before removal.
Loading Colors

items: Loading Background: Sets the background color shown during the loading state.
Loading Color: Sets the color of the loading indicator displayed while content is loading.

contents.content: Other Colors

items: Modal Overlay: Controls the color of the overlay displayed behind modal windows, popups, quick views, and other overlay-based components.

contents.content: Typography
Customize the fonts used throughout your store to create a consistent brand experience.
Fonts

items: Body: Select the font used for general content such as product descriptions, collection descriptions, paragraphs, buttons, and other standard text across the store.
Heading: Select the font used for headings and titles, including page titles, section headings, product titles, and collection titles.
Subheading: Select the font used for subheadings and secondary titles that appear between headings and body text.

contents.content: Layout
Control the overall spacing and width of your store's content across different screen sizes.
Spacing

items: Mobile horizontal padding: Sets the total horizontal spacing on mobile devices. The value is divided evenly between the left and right sides of the page. (Default: 40px)
Desktop horizontal padding: Sets the total horizontal spacing on desktop devices. The value is divided evenly between the left and right sides of the page. (Default: 60px)

Global maximum layout width: Defines the maximum width of the store's content area. Once the browser window exceeds this width, the content remains centered and will not continue expanding. (Default: 1920px)

contents.content: Product Card
Configure how product cards look and behave in your store.
Card Settings

items: Background color: Sets the background color of the product card. (Default: #F2F2F2)
Hover style: Controls the animation when customers hover over a product card. Options: Slide — content shifts with a sliding effect; Flip — the card flips to reveal more information.

Flip effect: Controls the animation style when Hover style is set to Flip. Options: Fade — smooth fade between front and back card content; Flip — card-flipping animation between front and back. This setting only applies when Hover style is set to Flip.
Reverse price order: Reverses the display order of product prices (for example, shows compare-at price before sale price, or vice versa).

contents.content: Badge Settings

Badges are displayed based on specific conditions. If a product has a compare-at price, a discount badge will be shown. If the product is new, a New badge will appear. If the product is out of stock, an Out of Stock badge will be displayed. For custom badges, use a metafield to define your own custom text.

items: Show badge: Show or hide the badge on product cards.
Background color: Badge background color. (Default: #1C1C1C)
Text color: Badge text color. (Default: #FFFFFF)
Text size: Badge text size in pixels. (Default: 14px)
Font weight: Thickness of badge text. Options: Regular, Medium, Semi bold, Bold.
Text transform: Controls the capitalization of badge text. Options: Lowercase, Uppercase, Capitalize.
Top padding: Space above the badge text. (Default: 4px)
Bottom padding: Space below the badge text. (Default: 4px)
Left padding: Space to the left of the badge text. (Default: 10px)
Right padding: Space to the right of the badge text. (Default: 10px)

contents.content: Card Button Settings
Control the product card's Quick view and Add to cart buttons.

items: Show Quick view: Show or hide the Quick view button on product cards.
Show Add to cart button: Show or hide the Add to cart button on product cards.
Reverse button order: Swap the positions of the Quick view and Add to cart buttons.

contents.content: Card Marquee Settings
Enable this to show product cards in a scrolling marquee. The text comes from the product's Marquee metafield. If the metafield is empty, the marquee won't display any content.

items: Show product marquee: Enable or disable the scrolling product marquee.
Vertical padding: Vertical spacing inside the marquee. (Default: 4rem)
Direction: Sets the scrolling direction of the marquee. Options: Auto, Right to left, Left to right.
Marquee speed: Controls how fast the marquee scrolls. A lower value is faster; a higher value is slower. (Default: 170s)
Item gap: Space between product cards in the marquee. (Default: 4rem)
Marquee background color: Background color behind the scrolling product cards. (Default: #000000)
Marquee text color: Text color for any text displayed inside the marquee. (Default: #FFFFFF)

contents.content: Cart
Control how the shopping cart behaves when customers add products to their cart.
Cart Drawer

Open cart drawer after add to cart: Enable this option to automatically open the cart drawer whenever a customer adds a product to their cart. This provides immediate feedback that the item has been added and allows customers to quickly review their cart contents without leaving the current page.

items: Enabled: The cart drawer opens automatically after each product is added to the cart.
Disabled: Products are added to the cart without opening the cart drawer. Customers can access their cart manually using the cart icon or cart page.

contents.content: Default: Disabled
Price
Control how product prices are displayed throughout your store.
Show Currency Codes

Show currency codes: Display the currency code alongside product prices across the storefront. Enabling this option helps customers clearly identify the currency being used, which is especially useful for stores selling to international audiences.

items: Enabled: Prices are displayed with the currency code (for example, $1.00 USD).
Disabled: Prices are displayed without the currency code (for example, $1.00).

contents.content: Default: Disabled
Social Links
Add links to your social media profiles to display social icons and help customers connect with your brand across different platforms.
Enter the URL for each social media profile you want to display. Leave a field blank to hide the corresponding social media icon from the storefront.
Social Accounts

items: Facebook: Add the URL of your Facebook page. When provided, a Facebook icon linking to your page will be displayed in supported areas of the storefront.
Instagram: Add the URL of your Instagram profile. When provided, an Instagram icon linking to your profile will be displayed in supported areas of the storefront.
Twitter / X: Add the URL of your Twitter (X) profile. When provided, a Twitter / X icon linking to your profile will be displayed in supported areas of the storefront.
YouTube: Add the URL of your YouTube channel. When provided, a YouTube icon linking to your channel will be displayed in supported areas of the storefront.

contents.content: More Platforms
Add URLs for additional social media platforms. Icons are only displayed for platforms that have a valid URL entered.

items: TikTok: Add the URL of your TikTok profile.
LinkedIn: Add the URL of your LinkedIn profile or company page.
Pinterest: Add the URL of your Pinterest profile.
WhatsApp: Add the URL for your WhatsApp contact or chat link.
Telegram: Add the URL of your Telegram profile, channel, or group.

contents.content: Colors Palette
Define a reusable set of brand colors available throughout the theme.
Color Palette

Define your store's color palette by entering color names and their corresponding color codes. Each color should be entered in the following format: Color Name:#HEXCODE — separate multiple colors with a comma.

contents.label: Guidelines
items: Use a descriptive color name followed by a colon (:) and the color's HEX code.
Separate each color entry with a comma (,).
HEX color codes should begin with # and contain 6 hexadecimal characters.
Valid example: Primary Blue:#478AFF, Secondary Gold:#D1A372, Accent Red:#FF0000
Invalid example: Primary Blue - #478AFF | Secondary Gold - #D1A372

contents.content: Announcement Bar
Display rotating promotional messages, contact information, and localization controls at the top of your storefront.

The Announcement Bar is a multifunctional header utility section designed to improve customer communication and enhance the shopping experience. It combines promotional messaging, contact information, and integrated language and currency selection using Shopify's Localization API. It is fully responsive and optimized for both desktop and mobile devices.
How to Edit the Announcement Bar

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
In the Theme Editor, select the Announcement Bar section from the left sidebar.
Add announcement blocks and adjust the available settings according to your needs.
Click Save to apply your changes.

contents.content: Section Settings
These settings control the overall appearance and functionality of the announcement bar.

items: Color Scheme: Selects the color scheme applied to the announcement bar.
Section Padding: Add padding at the top and bottom to fine-tune the spacing of the bar.
Contact Text: Displays contact information or a custom message on desktop devices.
Show Country Selector: Enables or disables the language and currency selector.
Autoplay: Automatically rotates announcement messages.
Autoplay Speed: Controls the delay between announcement transitions.

contents.content: Block Settings
The section supports up to 5 announcement blocks.

items: Message Text: The announcement message displayed within the slider.
URL: Optional destination link for the announcement message.

contents.content: Best Practices

contents.label: Do
items: Keep announcement messages short and easy to scan.
Keep contact information up to date.
Configure language and currency options when selling internationally.
Link announcements to relevant landing pages when applicable.

contents.label: Don't
items: Use excessively long announcement messages.
Use announcements for large amounts of content.
Enable localization selectors if Shopify Markets is not configured.
Overload customers with multiple competing announcements.

contents.content: Header
Configure the primary navigation area of your storefront, including menus, search, account access, and cart interactions.

The Header serves as the primary navigation area of the storefront, providing customers with access to navigation links, search functionality, customer accounts, and cart interactions. It is designed to improve product discovery, streamline navigation, and maintain quick access to key store features throughout the customer journey. The section supports customizable navigation menus, optional utility icons, sticky header functionality, search enhancements, and cart drawer upsell opportunities.
How to Edit the Header

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Select the Header section from the left sidebar.
Modify the settings according to your store requirements.
Click Save to apply the changes.

contents.content: Section Settings

items: Color Scheme: Select the color scheme applied to the header. This setting controls the overall visual appearance of the header and helps maintain consistency with your store branding.

Main Menu: Select the navigation menu displayed in the header. The selected menu serves as the primary navigation structure for your storefront and can be used to trigger mega menu content where applicable.
Show Search Icon: Displays a search icon within the header, allowing customers to quickly access the store search experience.
Show Account Icon: Displays a customer account icon that provides access to login, registration, and account management pages.
Show Cart Icon: Displays the cart icon and allows customers to access their shopping cart from any page.

Enable Cart Drawer Gift Box: When enabled, customers can add an optional gift product directly from the cart drawer before proceeding to checkout. This feature can be used to promote gift wrapping services, complimentary products, or special add-on items.
Cart Drawer Gift Product: Select the Signature gift box product that will be offered as the optional gift item within the cart drawer.

Enable Sticky Header: Keeps the header visible at the top of the page while customers scroll. This improves navigation accessibility and provides continuous access to search, account, and cart functionality throughout the shopping journey.
Suggested Searches: Enter popular search terms separated by commas. These suggestions help customers discover products more quickly and improve the overall search experience.
Example: Running Shoes, Sneakers, New Arrivals, Best Sellers.

Suggested Products Collection: Select a collection to display recommended products within the search interface. Products from the selected collection can be surfaced to customers during search interactions to improve product discovery and engagement.

contents.content: Best Practices

contents.label: Do
items: Use a clear and organized navigation structure.
Enable sticky header for improved usability.
Add relevant suggested search terms.
Use a high-converting collection for suggested products.
Enable the gift box feature when offering gift wrapping or add-on products.

contents.label: Don't
items: Use overly complex navigation menus.
Add unrelated search suggestions.
Offer unavailable products as gift options.
Disable essential icons that customers frequently use.

contents.content: Mega Menu
Create large dropdown navigation panels that display categories, brands, featured products, and promotional imagery.

The Mega Menu is a Header block that creates a wide dropdown navigation panel for specific menu items. Multiple Mega Menu blocks can be added within the Header section, allowing different navigation items to display their own unique content. It supports category links, brand navigation, featured product sliders, promotional banner images, and scrolling marquee messaging.
Adding a Mega Menu

items: Go to Shopify Admin → Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
Navigate to the Header section.
Click Add block and select Mega Menu.
Configure the settings below and click Save.

contents.content: Block Settings

contents.label: Trigger
items: Nav Link Title: Enter the exact navigation menu item name that should trigger this Mega Menu. The value must match the navigation item name exactly (case-insensitive). If no matching menu item is found, the Mega Menu will not appear. Example: if your main menu contains an item named Shop, enter Shop in this field.

contents.label: Column 1 – Categories
items: Column Heading: Enter the heading shown above the category links. Examples: Categories, Collections, Shop by Category.
Categories Menu: Select a Shopify menu containing category or collection links. When a link points to a collection, the product count is displayed automatically beside the collection name.

contents.label: Column 2 – Brands
items: Column Heading: Enter the heading shown above the brand links. Examples: Brands, Shop by Brand, Featured Brands.
Brands Menu: Select a Shopify menu containing brand links. Collection-based links automatically display the associated product count.

contents.label: Column 3 – Featured Products
items: Column Heading: Enter the heading shown above the featured product area. Examples: Featured, Best Sellers, Trending Now, New Arrivals.
Number of Products to Show: Choose how many products appear in the featured product slider. Range: 1–10 products.

Product List: Select the products to feature. Up to 10 products can be assigned; the display limit is controlled by the Number of Products to Show setting. The featured area displays product image, title, pricing, compare-at price (when applicable), and a direct product link.

contents.label: Column 4 – Mega Banner
items: Mega Banner Image: Upload an image to appear in the banner area. Recommended size: 790 × 499 px (approximately 3:2 landscape ratio). Portrait or square images may be cropped to fit.
Marquee Line 1: Enter the first promotional message to scroll over the banner image. Default: Check out our latest products!
Marquee Line 2: Enter the second promotional message. Default: Discover what's new in store! Both messages repeat in a continuous scrolling marquee.

contents.content: Best Practices

contents.label: Do
items: Match the Nav Link Title with the navigation item name exactly.
Organize categories and brands into separate menus.
Feature high-performing or seasonal products.
Use high-quality landscape banner images.
Keep marquee messages short and easy to read.
Create separate Mega Menu blocks for different navigation items when needed.

contents.label: Don't
items: Use a Nav Link Title that does not exist in the navigation menu.
Overload the featured product area with unrelated products.
Upload low-resolution banner images.
Use long promotional messages that are difficult to read while scrolling.
Leave important content areas empty if the Mega Menu is intended to drive product discovery.

contents.content: Footer
Configure the footer that appears on every page, providing customers with navigation links, policies, newsletter signup, and brand elements.

The Footer is an important part of your store layout as it appears on every page and provides customers with quick access to essential information and navigation links. It supports menus, newsletter signup, social media icons, branding elements like logos, and localization controls — helping you maintain a consistent brand identity across your store.
How to Edit the Footer

items: From your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
Click Customize on the active theme.
In the Theme Editor, scroll down and select the Footer section from the left sidebar.
Adjust the settings, blocks, and layout options according to your preferences.
Click Save to apply your changes.

contents.content: Section Settings

items: Color Scheme: Choose a color scheme to control the background and text colors of the footer section.
Select Background Image (Optional): Upload an image to display as the footer background for a more visually appealing design.
Background Image Overlay: Adjust the overlay opacity to improve text visibility when a background image is applied.
Top Menu: Select the menu that will appear at the top of the footer. Typically contains quick navigation links such as Contact, Blog, FAQs, or About pages.
Show Social Media: Enable this option to display social media icons in the footer linking to your social platforms.
Footer Logo Image: Upload a logo image to display in the footer.
Logo Position: Choose where the footer logo appears — Left, Center, or Right.
Logo Width: Adjust the size of the footer logo using the width slider.
Marquee Text: Add scrolling text to display promotional messages, announcements, or brand highlights in the footer area.
Apply Bottom Border: Enable this option to display a separator line between footer sections for a cleaner layout.
Block Heading Font Weight: Adjust the font thickness of the footer block headings.
Enable Follow on Shop: Allow customers to follow your store through the Shop app.
Show Localization: Displays language and country selectors so customers can change their region or language.
Show Payment Icons: Display accepted payment method icons such as Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and PayPal.
Bottom Logo: Upload an additional logo that will appear in the bottom section of the footer.
Section Spacing: Adjust the top and bottom spacing above and below the footer.

contents.content: Available Blocks

contents.label: Menu Block
items: Column Max Width: Adjust the width of the menu column to control how much space the block occupies in the footer.
Heading: Enter a title for the menu block. Examples: Collections, Quick Links, Policies.
Select Menu: Choose a navigation menu from your Shopify menu list to appear under this heading. Multiple Menu blocks can be added to display different groups of links.

contents.label: Newsletter Block
items: Column Max Width: Adjust the width of the newsletter column.
Heading: Enter a heading for the newsletter section. Example: Subscribe To Get 10% OFF.
Text: Add a short description encouraging visitors to subscribe, highlighting benefits such as exclusive updates or special discounts.
Apply Secondary Color Scheme: Enable this option to apply an alternative color scheme to the newsletter block for better visual emphasis.
Show Policy Info: Display additional policy information below the newsletter form, informing customers they accept the terms and privacy policy by entering their email.

contents.label: Text Block
items: Column Max Width: Adjust the width of the text block to control how much space it occupies in the footer layout.
Heading: Enter a title for the text block. Examples: About Us, Store Information, Contact Details.
Description: Add the text content that will appear under the heading — such as a short store description, contact information, or any additional message for your customers.

contents.content: Settings Page
Control the app's core conversion behavior, rate updates, and storefront recovery options.
General Tab

contents.label: General settings
items: Base Currency: Set the core currency used for all conversions.
App Behavior: Control the storefront wizard visibility.
Enable Currency Conversion: Unchecking this option will immediately stop all currency conversions on your storefront.
Remember Visitor Preference: Uses browser local storage to save the customer's preferred currency until they clear their cookies.
Exchange Rate Update: Rates sync every 6, 12, 24, or 48 hours based on your settings.

contents.content: Important Note: To restore Shopify's default currency behavior, you must revert the changes made to your Shopify Currency Formatting settings.

Restoring Defaults: If you choose to uninstall the app or want to return to your original store look, go to your Shopify Admin settings and clear all the input fields where you previously added the Rico code. Once these fields are empty and saved, Shopify will automatically revert to its native price formatting.
Danger Zone

contents.label: High impact actions
items: Reset All Settings: Restores all currency and widget settings to defaults. Clears active selections.
Clear Visitor Cookies: Removes saved preferences from all visitor browsers on their next page load.

contents.content: Widget Preview Tab
Use this tab to preview placement, control visibility, and match the widget to your brand.
Widget Placement

contents.label: Placement options
items: Positioning: Set the wizard position exactly where you want it.
Custom Injection: Use a specific CSS Class or ID to inject the widget into a custom part of your theme.
Edge Offsets: Adjust the distance from the screen edges for both mobile and desktop.

contents.content: Branding & Visibility

contents.label: Branding controls
items: Full Branding: Change the background and text colors to match your store's exact branding.
Device Visibility: Choose to hide the wizard on specific devices (for example, hide on mobile but show on desktop) while keeping currency conversion active.

contents.content: Privacy Policy
Effective Date: May 01, 2026
1. Introduction

Rico ("we," "our," or "the App") provides currency localization services for Shopify merchants store. We are committed to a privacy policy. Our app is built to function with minimal data interaction, ensuring a fast and secure experience for both merchants and their global customers.
2. Merchant Information (Data We Collect)
To provide our services, we collect limited administrative data from merchants:

items: Shopify Store ID and Domain: To identify your account and apply settings.
Contact Information: Email addresses provided via Shopify for support
Theme Access: We use "Theme Embed" permissions to render the currency switcher script on your storefront.

contents.content: 3. Customer Data & Geolocation

items: We do not collect, store, or share Personal Identifiable Information (PII) from your customers.

contents.content: Real-Time Geolocation: To display local pricing, the App processes the visitor's IP address in real-time. This processing happens "in-memory" to determine the country code and is not stored on our servers.
Browser Local Storage: We utilize the customer's browser local storage only to remember their preferred currency. This ensures a consistent experience as they navigate your store.

Smart Updates: While the App automatically detects location, customers always have the option to switch currencies manually. The local storage is automatically updated to reflect the most recent exchange rates based on merchant settings.
4. Global Regulatory Compliance
We adhere to the highest international data protection standards to ensure your store remains compliant and secure worldwide. Our operations are aligned with the following regulations:

GDPR (European Union): We operate as a Data Processor under the General Data Protection Regulation. We process data based on the "Legitimate Interest" of providing a functional, localized storefront. We do not transfer or sell European user data to any third parties. Official Reference: https://gdpr-info.eu/

CCPA/CPRA (United States): We do not "sell" or "share" customer personal information as defined by California law. Our app respects user privacy settings and allows customers to opt-out of tracking through your store's primary privacy banner. Official Reference: https://oag.ca.gov/privacy/ccpa
5. Data Security & Storage

Because we do not store customer profiles or order histories, the risk of data breach is significantly minimized. Merchant account data is stored on secure, encrypted servers and is only accessible by authorized personnel for support purposes.
6. Your Rights (Merchant & Customer)

Merchants: You may request to view, update, or delete your account data at any time by contacting us. And when you uninstall this app, we keep your data analytics for some time, but settings and other things are not kept.

Customers: Since we don't save any customer information, we don't have any records to show or delete. This means your customer data stays safe and compliant because there is no customer data for us to share or lose.
7. Data Security
At Rico, we prioritize the security of your store. Our infrastructure is built on the principle of Data Minimization.
Merchant Protection: We only store essential merchant data required to keep the service running. This information is encrypted and stored on secure servers with restricted access.

No Customer Data Collection: We do not collect, store, or log any personal information from your customers. While our app detects a visitor's location to switch currencies, this process happens instantly in real-time and is never saved to a database.
Safe & Non-Intrusive: Because we never touch customer PII (Personally Identifiable Information), your store remains fully compliant with global privacy standards without any extra risk.
8. Changes to this Policy
We may update this policy to reflect new global regulations. We encourage merchants to review this page periodically. Continued use of the App after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
9. Children's Privacy
Our service is designed exclusively for Shopify merchants and their commercial operations. Rico does not knowingly collect, request, or store any data from children.

Because our app functions strictly as a currency switching tool and does not require user accounts or personal profiles from store visitors, we do not process any "Personally Identifiable Information" (PII) from children or any other age group. We are fully committed to complying with the Children's Online Privacy.
10. Contact Us
If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your store's data, please contact our dedicated support team:

items: Developer: Nextvence
Support Email: svaomegaprime@gmail.com
Availability: 24/7 Excellence Support

contents.content: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Here are some frequently asked questions about Rico and how it works.

contents.heading: How does the app detect my customer's location?
contents.desc: Our app uses smart IP-based geolocation technology. When a customer visits your store, the app identifies their country in real-time and automatically switches the currency to match their local market, creating a seamless shopping experience.

contents.heading: Will the app slow down my store’s loading speed?
contents.desc: Not at all. We use a lightweight 'Theme Embed' script that loads asynchronously. This means it doesn't interfere with your store’s main content, ensuring your page speed remains fast and your SEO stays strong.

contents.heading: Can I customize the look of the currency switcher?
contents.desc: Yes! You have total control over the design. You can customize the colors to match your brand and choose the exact position where the widget appears on your site whether it's the header, footer, or a floating position.

contents.heading: What happens if a customer's currency isn't supported?
contents.desc: We provide a 'Fallback Currency' feature. If a visitor comes from a region where you haven't enabled a specific currency, the app will automatically display your chosen fallback currency (usually your store's base currency) so they always see a price.

contents.heading: How often are the exchange rates updated?
contents.desc: To ensure accuracy, our exchange rates are updated automatically in real-time. This protects your margins by ensuring that the converted prices always reflect the most current global market rates without any manual work from you.

contents.heading: Does the app store any customer personal data?
contents.desc: No. Privacy is our priority. We do not collect or store any Personally Identifiable Information (PII) from your customers. The geolocation process happens instantly and is never saved on our servers, making your store fully compliant with GDPR and CCPA.
